title: inula britannica subsp. hispanica
intro: Inula britannica subsp. hispanica, also known as Yellowhead or Spanish-Headed Inula, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This perennial plant is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Inula britannica subsp. hispanica include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular natural remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as teas and infusions, are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems, while also offering antioxidant and anti-bacterial benefits.
